1	package agent
2	
3	// execute_bead_land_test.go — Land() coordinator-pattern unit tests.
4	//
5	// These tests run against a real temp git repo so they exercise the actual
6	// git plumbing (update-ref, merge --no-ff, worktree add, etc.) rather than a
7	// mock. Each scenario asserts that the target tip advances correctly and —
8	// crucially for replay fidelity — that the worker's own commit is never
9	// rewritten. Its parent always stays BaseRev so replay sees the same inputs
10	// the worker saw at execution time.
11	
12	import (
13		"fmt"
14		"os"
15		"os/exec"
16		"path/filepath"
17		"strings"
18		"sync"
19		"testing"
20		"time"
21	)
22	
23	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
24	// Test helpers (real git repo fixtures)
25	// ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	type landTestRepo struct {
28		t       *testing.T
29		dir     string
30		origin  string // path to a bare origin repo, or "" if no remote
31		branch  string // target branch
32		baseSHA string // the initial commit on the target branch
33	}
34	
35	func newLandTestRepo(t *testing.T) *landTestRepo {
36		t.Helper()
37		dir := t.TempDir()
38		r := &landTestRepo{t: t, dir: dir, branch: "main"}
39		r.runGit("init", "-b", "main")
40		r.runGit("config", "user.name", "Test")
41		r.runGit("config", "user.email", "test@test.local")
42		r.writeFile("README.md", "# test\n")
43		r.runGit("add", "-A")
44		r.runGit("commit", "-m", "init")
45		r.baseSHA = r.resolveRef("refs/heads/main")
46		return r
47	}
48	
49	// newLandTestRepoWithOrigin creates a test repo whose origin is a separate
50	// bare repo. Used by the push-ff-only test.
51	func newLandTestRepoWithOrigin(t *testing.T) *landTestRepo {
52		t.Helper()
53		r := newLandTestRepo(t)
54	
55		bareDir := t.TempDir()
56		cmd := exec.Command("git", "init", "--bare", "-b", "main", bareDir)
57		if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
58			t.Fatalf("git init --bare: %s: %v", string(out), err)
59		}
60		r.origin = bareDir
61		r.runGit("remote", "add", "origin", bareDir)
62		r.runGit("push", "-u", "origin", "main")
63		return r
64	}
65	
66	func (r *landTestRepo) runGit(args ...string) string {
67		r.t.Helper()
68		cmd := exec.Command("git", append([]string{"-C", r.dir}, args...)...)
69		out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
70		if err != nil {
71			r.t.Fatalf("git %s: %s: %v", strings.Join(args, " "), string(out), err)
72		}
73		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
74	}
75	
76	func (r *landTestRepo) writeFile(path, content string) {
77		r.t.Helper()
78		full := filepath.Join(r.dir, path)
79		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(full), 0o755); err != nil {
80			r.t.Fatal(err)

19	// TestMain scrubs all GIT_* environment variables before running tests so
20	// test subprocesses don't inherit lefthook's GIT_DIR/GIT_WORK_TREE and leak
21	// into the parent repo's config.
22	func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
23		for _, kv := range os.Environ() {
24			if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
25				if idx := strings.IndexByte(kv, '='); idx >= 0 {
26					_ = os.Unsetenv(kv[:idx])
27				}
28			}
29		}
30		os.Exit(m.Run())
31	}
32

63	  path: "./library"
64	  repository:
65	    url: "https://github.com/test/repo"
66	    branch: "main"
67	bead:
68	  backend: %s
69	`, backend)
70		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(content), 0o644))
71	}

1928	// pickerSkip records a single bead that the picker passed over while looking
1929	// for the next candidate. It is used to emit a structured picker.priority_skip
1930	// event when a worker claims a lower-priority bead while a higher-priority
1931	// bead was skipped, so future starvation regressions are observable.
1932	//
1933	// Reason values are bounded to the set named in ddx-9d55601f AC #4:
1934	//
1935	//	label_filter, in_attempted, claim_race, eligibility_filter, retry_cooldown
1936	//
1937	// Note: eligibility_filter and retry_cooldown are applied upstream in
1938	// Store.ReadyExecution (so beads filtered for those reasons never reach
1939	// nextCandidate at all). They are part of the reason vocabulary so future
1940	// picker rearrangements can re-emit them without changing the schema.
1941	type pickerSkip struct {
1942		BeadID   string
1943		Priority int
1944		BeadRank *int
1945		Reason   string
1946	}
1947	
1948	func hasGuardSkips(skips []pickerSkip) bool {
1949		for _, skip := range skips {
1950			switch skip.Reason {
1951			case "label_filter", "in_attempted", "claim_race", "eligibility_filter", "retry_cooldown", "target_bead":
1952				continue
1953			default:
1954				return true
1955			}
1956		}
1957		return false
1958	}
1959	
1960	func queueRankValue(raw any) any {
1961		rank, ok := parseQueueRank(raw)
1962		if !ok {
1963			return nil
1964		}
1965		return rank
1966	}
1967

176	// TestExternalBackendOpensBeadsArchiveWithFallback verifies that opening the
177	// "beads-archive" collection through the bd external backend produces a

199		require.Equal(t, "bd", backend.Tool)

22	type ArchivePolicy struct {
23		// Statuses lists the bead statuses that are eligible for archival.
24		Statuses []string
25		// MinAge is the minimum time since a bead was last updated/closed
26		// before it may be archived.

29		// archival is a no-op. Set to 0 to archive regardless of size.

35	// DefaultArchivePolicy returns the shipping defaults from TD-027 §(b).
36	func DefaultArchivePolicy() ArchivePolicy {
37		return ArchivePolicy{
38			Statuses:       []string{StatusClosed},
39			MinAge:         30 * 24 * time.Hour,
40			MinActiveCount: 2000,
41			BatchSize:      500,
42		}
43	}

49		return NewStoreWithCollection(s.Dir, BeadsArchiveCollection)

1111	func (s *Store) Close(id string) error {
1112		if err := s.SetLifecycleStatus(id, StatusClosed, LifecycleTransitionOptions{
1113			ManualClose: true,
1114			Reason:      "manual close",
1115			Source:      "Store.Close",
1116		}); err != nil {
1117			return err
1118		}
1119		if err := s.externalizeEvents(id); err != nil {
1120			return err
1121		}
1122		_ = s.RemoveClaimHeartbeat(id)
1123		s.maybeOpportunisticMaintenance()
1124		return nil
1125	}
1126	
1127	// externalizeEvents moves a bead's inline events into its sidecar attachment
1128	// under a fresh lock. Safe to call repeatedly — no-op when there are no
1129	// inline events (already externalized, or none recorded).
1130	func (s *Store) externalizeEvents(id string) error {
1131		if s.axonGraphQLActive() {
1132			return nil
1133		}
1134		return s.WithLock(func() error {
1135			beads, _, err := s.readAllLatestRaw()
1136			if err != nil {
1137				return err
1138			}
1139			for i := range beads {
1140				if beads[i].ID != id {
1141					continue
1142				}
1143				if err := s.externalizeEventsInPlace(&beads[i]); err != nil {
1144					return err
1145				}
1146				return s.WriteAll(beads)
1147			}
1148			return nil
1149		})
1150	}
1151	
1152	// ErrClosureGateRejected indicates a close was refused because the bead does
1153	// not carry the evidence required for an automated closure: a terminal
1154	// verdict event (review APPROVE with non-empty rationale, or an explicit
1155	// review-skipped / manual-close marker) AND an execution-evidence marker
1156	// (closing_commit_sha, session_id, or an execute-bead success event in the
1157	// events history).
1158	//
1159	// Automated execute-loop paths (execute-bead + reviewer) always go through
1160	// CloseWithEvidence. The plain Store.Close remains as the manual
1161	// administration escape hatch — it bypasses the gate by design (ddx-e30e60a9
1162	// acceptance §1).
1163	var ErrClosureGateRejected = fmt.Errorf("closure gate: insufficient evidence")
1164	
1165	// ClosureGate inspects a bead and returns nil iff the close is safe. It
1166	// rejects only the specific shapes that caused the 2026-04-18/20
1167	// review-malfunction incidents:
1168	//
1169	//  1. axon-c5cc071a (silent false-closure): closed with no events AND no
1170	//     closing_commit_sha. Rejected by the execution-evidence check —
1171	//     closing_commit_sha must be non-empty OR at least one event must exist.
1172	//  2. f7ae036f (broken APPROVE): a review event with summary=APPROVE whose
1173	//     body is empty. Rejected — APPROVE with no rationale is exactly the
1174	//     parse-mis-extract shape the reviewer bug produced.
1175	//
1176	// Beads without a review step (--no-review, no Reviewer configured, already-
1177	// satisfied paths) pass the gate as long as they carry execution evidence.
1178	// This keeps the surface small: the gate catches known-bad shapes, not every
1179	// conceivable edge case. Additional invariants belong in future dedicated
1180	// beads so the rejection reason stays auditable.

data.text: All checks pass. Now commit:

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(agent/land): SyncWorkTreeToHead unconditionally skips beads JSONL files

.ddx/beads.jsonl and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl are live-state files
governed by bead.Store WithLock. The committed worktree snapshot is
stale (agent claim-time state); the main worktree copy is always
authoritative. Add alwaysSkipSyncPaths so SyncWorkTreeToHead never
overwrites them regardless of dirty-before-land state.

Add regression tests TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsJSONL
and TestSyncWorkTreeToHead_DoesNotClobberBeadsArchiveJSONL.

Add TestQueueRank_* lifecycle tests verifying queue-rank is preserved
across Claim, Unclaim, Close, Reopen, ReconcileLifecycleMetadata,
MigrateLifecycle, ArchiveWithEvents, and JSONL round-trip (both int
and string forms). [ddx-2257460f]
EOF
)"
